prevalence.value: 72.2% (any lipid abnormality, including high LDL-C, low HDL-C, high TG, or high non-HDL-C)
prevalence.trend: Increasing by ~2-3% annually due to urbanization and dietary shifts

incidence.value: Not routinely tracked; estimated 5-7% new cases annually in at-risk populations
incidence.population: Adults aged 20-60 with metabolic risk factors
incidence.source: Extrapolated from POSHAN and ICMR data

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; ~10-15% of CVD deaths linked (1.2 million CVD deaths/year)

riskFactors: Unhealthy diet high in saturated fats, trans fats, and refined carbs (e.g., ghee, fried foods, low fiber intake)
Genetic predisposition (e.g., higher prevalence of familial hypercholesterolemia in South Indian and Parsi populations; APOE gene variants)
vulnerablePopulations: South Indians (higher genetic and dietary risks)
Individuals with family history of CVD
Low socioeconomic groups with poor access to healthy diets
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Per Lipid Association of India (LAI) guidelines (2017, updated 2022): Fasting lipid profile - TC >200 mg/dL, LDL-C >130 mg/dL (or >100 in high-risk), HDL-C <40 mg/dL (men)/<50 (women), TG >150 mg/dL. Non-fasting options for screening.

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: <20% of adults receive regular lipid screening (higher in urban areas ~30%)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~15-20% achieve target lipid levels with treatment

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted LAI guidelines with lower BMI-adjusted cutoffs for Indians (e.g., LDL-C target <100 mg/dL for all due to higher CVD risk at lower levels)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle modification first (DASH-like diet, exercise); pharmacotherapy with statins (atorvastatin/rosuvastatin), fibrates for TG; per NPCDCS program under NHM
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rural-urban disparity in access; cultural dietary preferences (e.g., high-carb vegetarian diets exacerbating TG); low awareness and adherence; counterfeit drugs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Integration with NCD programs like NPCDCS; AYUSHMAN Bharat coverage for statins; growing telemedicine for urban-rural screening
